text: The patient is positioned supine with legs apart on the operating table, which is set in a Trendelenburg position with a right lateral inclination. The surgeon uses eight-millimeter trocars and one twelve-millimeter assistant trocar to mobilize the sigmoid colon from the left abdominal wall, followed by mobilization of the sigmoid mesentery via a medial approach.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery at its origin. An improper dissection with a permanent cautery hook leads to vascular avulsion at the artery's root. Compression hemostasis is applied using fenestrated bipolar forceps. The cautery hook is replaced with a needle driver, and a figure-eight suture combined with a running suture is performed on the bleeding site using a 4-0 Prolene suture.
The surgeon reinforces the previously sutured bleeding site with an additional 4-0 Prolene suture, ensuring hemostasis as no active bleeding is observed.
